title: Anteriorchis × kallithea
intro: Anteriorchis × kallithea, also known as the Kallithea orchid, is a hybrid plant species within the orchid family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Anteriorchis × kallithea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of mild infections.
taxonomy: Anteriorchis × kallithea is a hybrid orchid species. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Anteriorchis is part of the subtribe Orchidinae within the tribe Orchideae. The species is native to the island of Cyprus. It is classified as an endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
